acceptance_checks_for_revision: Uploaded pbf_process_window.xlsx parses with openpyxl (no parsing error).
Formulas sheet present and contains entries for normalization, severity proxy, observed-risk proxy, classification bands, sensitivity logic, and budget checks.
qualification_note.md updated with explicit mapping checklist and references to attached CSV/formulas_export files.
extra_model_fields.estimated_effort: low (resave workbook, add sheet, export CSVs, update note)
extra_model_fields.expected_outcome: Automated graders and verifiers will be able to parse the workbook and confirm formulas reference input fields, restoring full deterministic credit.
technical_notes: Ensure the XLSX is not password protected and does not rely on external links that block parsers.
If using formulas that are Excel-specific, test openpyxl on a dev environment to confirm it can read formula text; otherwise provide formulas_export.csv.

planned_changes: Resave the existing pbf_process_window.xlsx in a standard XLSX format compatible with openpyxl (Office Excel 'Save As -> Excel Workbook (*.xlsx)').
Add a new worksheet named 'Formulas' that lists critical formula cells and their full formula text (columns: sheet!cell, formula_text, purpose).
Export the calculation, sensitivity, and budget-check sheets as CSV files (calculations_sheet.csv, sensitivity_sheet.csv, budget_sheet.csv) and attach them to the attempt.

Update qualification_note.md with a mapping checklist that ties each packet field to the exact workbook ranges/cells (e.g., 'RawData!B2:B5 -> laser_power_W for R1..R4', 'Calc!E2 -> observed_risk_proxy formula referencing Calc!B2..D2').

Where possible, replace any hard-coded numeric literals in calculation sheets with cell references to a constants sheet (e.g., Threshold cell, Remediation budget cell) and document those cell addresses in the note.

Run a local verification using openpyxl (or include a minimal Python snippet in the submission) that reads the workbook and prints the formulas referenced in the 'Formulas' sheet to demonstrate parseability.
raw_llm_output.revision_reason: Remove the remaining verification ambiguity by providing machine-readable proof of in-sheet formulas and explicit mappings from packet fields to workbook ranges so graders can programmatically confirm formula presence and references.

metadata_json.revision_goal: Deliverables present and clearly documented in qualification_note.md. Primary gap: the grader and verifier could not programmatically parse or inspect the spreadsheet (openpyxl unavailable), so the claim that formula cells were written and executable in-sheet remains unverified. Provide machine-readable proof that formulas reference input packet fields (not hard-coded constants) to remove the remaining uncertainty.
